sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player (N, 12 pixels) is now at rows 36-39, cols 24-26. The white strip (W, 4 pixels) is at col 27, rows 36-39 - to the RIGHT of the player.
The player moved from cols 13-15 to cols 24-26, that's 11 columns to the right. The player passed through the left box's columns (16-19) without teleporting!
Wait, but earlier when the player was at rows 28-30 and moved RIGHT into col 16, it teleported. Now the player is at rows 36-39 (below the box's rows 28-31) and moved RIGHT through cols 16-19 without teleporting.
So the teleport only happens when the player's rows overlap with the box's rows AND the player moves into the box's columns.
Now the player is at rows 36-39, cols 24-26, which is to the right of the left box. The strip is to the right of the player.
The left box is still 12 pixels. Nothing changed with the box.
Let me reconsider the whole approach. Maybe I need to go back to the center bar. The center bar is at rows 28-31, cols 28-39. The player is at rows 36-39, cols 24-26. The player is below and to the left of the center bar.
Maybe the goal is to interact with the center bar next? Let me try moving RIGHT to get below the center bar, then UP, SPACE, etc.
Actually, wait. Let me think about what happened with the right box:
1. Player was below right box
2. Pressed SPACE x3 to extend strip upward
3. Moved LEFT, which triggered the box to grow
For the left box:
1. Player was below left box (after teleport)
2. Pressed SPACE x3 but strip didn't extend much
3. Moved LEFT, but box didn't grow
The difference might be that for the right box, the strip was extending upward (player moved UP first), while for the left box, the strip was to the side.
Let me try again: get below the left box, move UP (strip above), SPACE x3, then RIGHT (to push strip into box).
But to get below the left box, I need to be at cols 16-19. Currently I'm at cols 24-26. I need to move LEFT.
Let me move LEFT to get back below the left box.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at rows 36-39, cols 24-26. Left box at rows 28-31, cols 16-19.
# Need to get to cols 16-19 (below the box). Move LEFT x6 to get to cols 18-20, then LEFT x2 more.
# Actually, player is 3 cols wide (24-26). Box is 4 cols wide (16-19).
# To be centered below, need cols 17-19 or 16-18.
# From cols 24-26, need LEFT x6 to get to cols 18-20, then LEFT x2 to get to cols 16-18.
# But player rows (36-39) don't overlap with box rows (28-31), so no teleport.
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
